Most reality capture hardware looks impressive in demos—but very few hold up on real projects. What separates the best systems right now? ✔️ Accurate data you can actually trust ✔️ Outputs that fit real BIM/VDC workflows ✔️ Fast capture without workflow friction That’s why devices like the XGRIDS Lixel K2 are getting attention. The industry is shifting from “cool scans” → usable project data. And the teams that adapt early will move faster than everyone else. How are you using reality capture today? 👇 Send us a message or book a time here: https://bit.ly/4uA8xeM

Precision Reality Capture to Power Every Project
About us
Precision Reality Capture to Power Every Project. Quantum Reality Capture (QRC) delivers high-accuracy LiDAR, drone, and as-built reality capture services that turn real-world spaces into precise, actionable digital data for AEC and real estate professionals, enabling better planning, design, and decision-making.

The pace of innovation in reality capture right now is hard to ignore. Tools like the XGRIDS Lixel K2 aren’t just improving how we scan—they’re redefining what we do with spatial data. We’re moving beyond static captures into workflows that actually support real-time coordination, clearer visualization, and better project decision-making. That shift—from data collection to usable intelligence—is where things get interesting. For AEC teams, this means: • Faster understanding of existing conditions • Better collaboration across stakeholders • More confidence in what’s being built vs. what’s designed We’re not just capturing reality anymore—we’re starting to work inside it. Curious how this could fit into your workflow? Let’s connect or drop a comment! We'd love to share what we’re seeing in the field. More to come as we keep digging into what this means in practice. #XGRIDS #LixelK2 #SpatialIntelligence #RealityCapture #3DScanning #DigitalTwin
What defines the gold standard in lightweight scanning? With Lixel K2, the standard is defined by three fundamentals: • Data Fidelity Engineering-grade point clouds accurate to 1 cm. Clean, consistent, and reliable — even in complex or low-feature environments. • Scene Versatility One scan, multiple outputs. From real-time and processed point clouds to mesh and 3D Gaussian Splatting, K2 adapts to diverse workflows — from urgent field delivery to high-fidelity visualization. • Ecosystem Integration From Scan-to-BIM and digital twins to simulation and beyond, K2 connects seamlessly into a full spatial intelligence pipeline, turning data capture into project delivery. All it takes is one lightweight device to open the door to spatial intelligence, that is Lixel K2 — the gold standard in lightweight scanning. Explore more about Lixel K2 here: https://lnkd.in/g3T2yW_Y #XGRIDS #LixelK2 #3DGS #SpatialIntelligence #RealityCapture #DigitalTwin #ScanToBIM #LiDAR #ConstructionTech #VirtualProduction #GaussianSplatting #3DScanning

Quantum Reality Capture reposted this
Most teams aren’t asking if they should use reality capture anymore. They’re asking: • How fast can we get usable data? • How do we reduce rework? • Should we bring this in-house? That’s the shift. Because the problem isn’t capturing reality…It’s how long it takes to turn that data into decisions. Here’s what we’re seeing across AEC: • Teams still relying on outdated site documentation • Data getting captured—but not actually used • Delays between field and office slowing everything down Meanwhile, the teams pulling ahead are doing this differently: • Capturing sites in hours, not days • Delivering outputs that plug directly into workflows • Using reality capture across the full project lifecycle—not just at the start That’s where the real advantage shows up: • Less rework • Faster coordination • Better decisions earlier We put together a quick FAQ carousel based on the conversations we’re having every week. If you’ve been asking any of these questions—you’re not alone. But you also don’t want to be late to this shift. If you’re looking at: • Bringing scanning in-house • Speeding up capture-to-decision time • Or just understanding what this should look like for your team Let’s connect. DM me if you want to see what this actually looks like in practice. #RealityCapture #LiDAR #DigitalTwin #ConstructionTech #AEC #BIM #VDC #Innovation #XGRIDS #QuantumRealityCapture

It was a great opportunity to connect with industry leaders, explore new ideas, and stay close to where design and construction workflows are heading. Looking forward to continuing the conversations and bringing those insights back into our work. If you’re exploring new approaches to reality capture or project workflows, we’d be happy to connect! We’ve cut capture time by up to 50%. And that changes everything in the field. Speed has always been one of the biggest constraints in reality capture. Tight timelines and limited site access mean that when capture is delayed, everything downstream gets pushed—coordination, design, and decision-making all depend on how quickly accurate data is available. We’re integrating a new approach to capturing environments using mobile LiDAR systems from XGRIDS. The result: ✅ Capture at walking speed ✅ Less time spent on-site ✅ Consistent, high-quality data across the entire environment But the real impact goes beyond speed: • Faster turnaround from field to deliverables • Reduced rework from missing or incomplete data • Stronger alignment across teams working from the same dataset We’re seeing a shift—from static, one-time capture to more continuous, efficient workflows. That’s where the real gains start to show up. Curious to learn more? Book a time here: https://bit.ly/4vFej0a
-
Big recognition for technology we’ve been working closely with. Congrats to XGRIDS on PortalCam being named Product of the Year across four categories at NAB Show 2026 👏 As a proud retailer of XGRIDS, it’s exciting to see this level of validation. We continue to be impressed with how the PortalCam enhances building and project imaging capabilities for AEC and Real-estate professionals across the industry. We’re seeing the same capabilities translate into: ✅ Faster capture workflows ✅ More immersive and accurate visual outputs ✅ More efficient ways to move from reality → usable data This is where things start to get interesting for industries beyond media. Curious how this could apply to your projects, or want to see the award-winning PortalCam in action in person? Schedule a meeting or lunch-and-learn with us—we’d love to walk you through it! Book a meeting here 👉 http://bit.ly/4tZgxWi
🎉 XGRIDS PortalCam Wins Product Of the Year in 4 Categories at NAB Show 2026!🎉 We are beyond excited and honored to announce that XGRIDS's PortalCam, the first true Spatial Camera, has won Product Of the Year in four categories at NAB Show 2026 in Las Vegas , one of the media and entertainment industry’s most competitive awards programs! All four categories are: • Intelligent Technology • Graphics, Editing, VFX & Switches • Cameras • Remote Production This award recognizes the impact our Spatial Intelligence technology has had in transforming virtual production, film, and content creation. XGRIDS has been at the forefront of revolutionizing how we capture the real world in 3D and bring it into immersive environments. From film production to virtual reality, our PortalCam has rapidly connected with professionals and prosumers alike, making high quality Gaussian Splatting capture broadly accessible, driving more efficient, scalable, and immersive workflows. 🚀 A huge thank you to NAB Show 2026 for recognizing PortalCam as a representation of cutting-edge Spatial Intelligence technology. Over the past few years, XGRIDS has meticulously developed our spatial computing stack, from scanners and file formats to SDKs. We’re excited to continue pushing the boundaries — stay tuned! #NABShow2026 #3DGS #VirtualProduction #VP #Filmmaking #SpatialComputing #SpatialIntelligence #RealityCapture #GaussianSplatting #CreatorTools #DigitalProduction #XGRIDS
-
We’ve been getting a lot of the same questions around reality capture lately. Not just about the technology itself, but how it actually fits into real projects. Things like: → Do we need a team every time we need a scan? → What do we actually get after? → How accurate is the data? → Can this fit into our current workflow? As more teams start exploring reality capture, these questions are coming up more often. And for good reason. The way projects are documented, measured, and managed is evolving. Teams are looking for faster, more reliable ways to move from site data to decisions, especially with newer solutions like XGRIDS. This is where reality capture starts to become more than just a tool. It becomes part of the workflow. If you’ve been asking similar questions, you’re not alone.

We’re live at the Future of Design & Construction Summit in Arizona! Big theme so far: the industry is moving faster—and workflows are finally catching up. Brooks Johnson and Todd Jarvis are onsite connecting with leaders and digging into how teams are actually building smarter today. If you’re here, we'd love to connect! Feel free to grab time with us here: https://bit.ly/4vFej0a
We’re heading to the Future of Design & Construction Summit next week in Arizona! Brooks Johnson and Todd Jarvis will be there connecting with industry leaders, learning from peers, and staying close to where the industry is heading. It’s always a great opportunity to step back, have real conversations, and see how teams are approaching design, construction, and project workflows today. If you’ll be attending, we’d love to connect! Whether it’s talking through current projects, sharing insights, or just meeting face-to-face, feel free to schedule some time with Todd here: http://bit.ly/4vFej0a See you there!
